fixture_scope: Demo-only user credentials seeded at server boot by #A-6 (model_inference_auth_seed_demo_users). Plaintext passwords are acceptable ONLY because the backend hashes them via password_hash(PASSWORD_DEFAULT) before INSERT, and because these are public, documented demo credentials — never reuse them for anything real.
honesty_rules: Plaintext passwords live here for reproducible demo onboarding. Identical treatment to the video-chat demo's admin@intelligent-intern.com / admin123 fixture — see demo/video-chat/backend-king-php/support/database.php lines 95-233.
Every field is env-overridable via MODEL_INFERENCE_DEMO_<USER>_USERNAME / _PASSWORD / _DISPLAY_NAME / _ROLE. The seed routine reads env first, falls back to this fixture.

Seeding is idempotent. A re-run that finds an existing user with the same username + a password_hash that verifies against the plaintext here is a no-op. A hash mismatch means the user rotated their password out-of-band — the seed MUST NOT overwrite in that case.
role must be one of {user, admin}. Flat role model — the demo intentionally does NOT ship the full video-chat RBAC matrix.

These credentials are the demo's public default. Hosting this demo on anything resembling a production surface requires rotating all three via the env overrides and disabling the fixture file via the MODEL_INFERENCE_AUTH_DISABLE_DEMO_SEED env flag (also introduced by #A-6).
Adding or removing users requires a corresponding edit to the auth-seed-contract test so the fixture stays in lockstep with its pinned expectations.

users.note: Full-access fixture for the demo. Not production-hardened.
Primary end-user fixture used by auth-smoke.sh and the A-batch Playwright cross-device test.
Second end-user fixture — used by conversation-ownership-contract to prove cross-user 403 ownership_denied.
